title: Meta pauses employee computer-use tracking for AI training
friendlyTitle: Meta paused a program that tracked employee computer activity for AI
summary: Meta paused an internal program reportedly designed to collect employee computer-use data, including keystrokes, mouse clicks, and screen content, for AI model training. The pause followed employee privacy concerns and a petition signed by more than 1,600 workers.
friendlySummary: Meta stopped an internal AI training project after workers objected to data collection from company laptops, including screen and input activity.
whyItMatters: As companies seek better agent-training data, workplace activity becomes an attractive but sensitive source. This is a clear warning that AI training programs need explicit consent, strict access controls, data minimization, and employee trust before they monitor real work.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This shows why AI training data rules matter at work. Helpful AI should not require turning employees’ screens into a fishbowl.
